fnmatch/translate.name: translate
fnmatch/translate.description: Return the shell-style pattern pat converted to a regular expression for using with re.match(). The pattern is expected to be a str. Example: >>> import fnmatch, re >>> >>> regex = fnmatch.translate('*.txt') >>> regex '(?s:.*\\.txt)\\z' >>> reobj = re.compile(regex) >>> reobj.match('foobar.txt') <re.Match object; span=(0, 10), match='foobar.txt'>
